params.name: Aeschylus Statue
params.description:
On Apr 30, 456 BC, the ancient Greeks erected this statue in remembrance of Aeschylus.
The archetype of this statue is Aeschylus, an ancient Greek tragedian. He has written more than 90 tragedies in his life, and only seven have been handed down. These compelling works are captivating in language, sublime in style, and show intense conflicts in concise plots. He also introduced a second actor in the drama performance, which changed the traditional performance mode, provided more possibilities …
